label: clean_trivial_pr
summary: The diff is correctly formatted and all three acceptance criteria are satisfied on the surface — the entry is present, existing commands are untouched, and formatting matches. However, the Executor's load-bearing assumption ('package.json already defines the lint:fix script') is verifiably false: package.json at the harness-ts root defines six scripts and lint:fix is not among them. The README now documents a command that will fail at runtime. Merging documentation for a non-existent script is a correctness defect: developers will read the README, run the command, and get an npm error. The fix is straightforward — add the script to package.json — but that work was not done. Additionally, no project-level README.md was found at the harness-ts root outside of node_modules, raising a secondary concern that the target file may have been newly created rather than modified; the diff context (hunk at line 14) implies prior content exists, but this could not be verified from the filesystem.

findings.description: The `lint:fix` script does not exist in package.json — the `scripts` section defines only `build`, `start`, `test`, `test:watch`, `test:e2e`, and `lint`; running `npm run lint:fix` will fail with 'Missing script: lint:fix'.
findings.suggestion: Add a `lint:fix` script to package.json (e.g. `"lint:fix": "tsc --noEmit"` if a formatter like prettier or eslint --fix is intended) before or alongside this README entry, or remove the README entry until the script is defined.

findings.description: The new `lint:fix` entry is inserted between `npm test` and `npm run build` rather than adjacent to the existing `npm run lint` entry; grouping lint-related commands together would improve scannability.
findings.suggestion: Move the `lint:fix` line to follow `npm run lint` so the two lint commands are consecutive.

fullReview.summary: The diff is correctly formatted and all three acceptance criteria are satisfied on the surface — the entry is present, existing commands are untouched, and formatting matches. However, the Executor's load-bearing assumption ('package.json already defines the lint:fix script') is verifiably false: package.json at the harness-ts root defines six scripts and lint:fix is not among them. The README now documents a command that will fail at runtime. Merging documentation for a non-existent script is a correctness defect: developers will read the README, run the command, and get an npm error. The fix is straightforward — add the script to package.json — but that work was not done. Additionally, no project-level README.md was found at the harness-ts root outside of node_modules, raising a secondary concern that the target file may have been newly created rather than modified; the diff context (hunk at line 14) implies prior content exists, but this could not be verified from the filesystem.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: README.md contains the new `npm run lint:fix` command under the Commands section
criteriaAssessment.evidence: The diff adds `- \`npm run lint:fix\` — fix lint errors automatically` within the Commands section between the `npm test` and `npm run build` entries.
The diff is a pure insertion (+1 line); the three existing command lines (`npm test`, `npm run build`, `npm run lint`) appear as context lines with no modification.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Spelling and formatting match existing command entries
criteriaAssessment.evidence: The new entry follows the identical pattern of existing entries: `- \`npm run <command>\` — <description>` with an em dash separator and lowercase description.
